texto: I loved a woman whose beauty Like the moon moved all the humming heavens to music till the stars with their tiny teeth burst into song and I fell on the ground before her while the sky hardened and she laughed and turned me down softly, I was so young.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Peter Meinke: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I loved a woman whose beauty Like the moon moved all the humming heavens to music till the stars …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, love, poetry pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de poetry e rejection.
